rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees referring to ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Respect AADL scope rules: component type contracts should not reference implementation-specific subcomponent ports directly.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annexes only in valid scopes and avoiding package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or helpers declared in the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; use implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
AADL property references in AGREE expressions
Defensive rules for type mismatches and invented constraints
